name: cpu_hpc_fft
title: Scientific compute kernel
short_description: Double precision FFT and dense linear algebra across all cores.

structured_text.description: Runs a mix of double precision fast Fourier transforms and dense matrix work across all cores using a tuned numerical library. The problem sizes are chosen to spill out of cache so the test exercises both arithmetic and memory traffic, which is how real simulations behave.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is sustained double precision throughput, reported in GFLOPS, averaged over the run.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More throughput means larger models and longer simulations finish in reasonable time.
structured_text.reasoning: Transforms and linear algebra are the building blocks of most numerical and engineering codes, so double precision throughput here predicts real scientific performance.

domain_gaming.explanation: Games use little double precision math.

domain_office.explanation: Office work has no heavy numerical component.

domain_software_development.explanation: Developers of numerical software benefit, but general coding does not.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Image work uses single precision math rather than this kernel.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Editing rarely touches double precision numerics.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Physical simulation for animation shares these transform and solver kernels.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Transforms here mirror the spectral math used in audio analysis.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Engineering simulation leans directly on double precision transforms and linear algebra.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: This is the core workload for numerical simulation and high performance computing.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Some scientific training and feature pipelines reuse these dense kernels.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Numerical preprocessing for models uses similar linear algebra.

domain_data_science.explanation: Statistical methods and signal analysis rely on transforms and matrix math.

domain_server_virt.explanation: General servers run little sustained double precision math.
